audit_items.proof_trace: The `cork_dllist_foreach_void` macro pre-fetches `_next = curr->next` before the loop body executes, so freeing `server` (which calls `cork_dllist_remove` then `ss_free`) does not invalidate the iterator. `remote` is captured from `server->remote` before `close_and_free_server` is called, and `free_server` only nullifies `remote->server` (back-reference) without freeing `remote`, so the subsequent `close_and_free_remote(loop, remote)` operates on valid memory. Each server owns a unique remote (1:1 allocation, never shared), ruling out double-free; `close_and_free_remote(loop, NULL)` is a safe no-op for servers that never acquired a remote.
